summary: This cluster investigates the evolutionary genomics of red algae, specifically focusing on structural variations in mitochondrial genomes. Researchers analyzed 42 mitogenomes from the Ceramiales, a highly diverse order comprising 2,669 species, and identified four novel architectures (A, B, C, and D) representing the first documented instances of such rearrangements in Rhodophyta. Phylogenetic analyses of protein-coding genes supported existing familial classifications, indicating that mitogenome architecture predates phylogenetic relationships and that palindromic and inverted repeat patterns may drive this structural variation. The findings underscore the role of mitochondrial genome rearrangements in understanding red algal diversity and evolutionary history, with a taxonomic scope centered on the Ceramiales.
